message:
fcp-core/policy: drop posture is_empty short-circuit that bypassed verifier+age constraints

`PolicyEngine::evaluate_invoke` was gating the posture check on
`posture_requirements.is_empty()`:

    if let Some(ref posture_requirements) = self.zone_policy.requires_posture {
        if !posture_requirements.is_empty() {
            if let Some(reason) = check_posture(posture_requirements, input) {
                return PolicyDecision::deny(reason, Vec::new());
            }
        }
    }

`PostureRequirements::is_empty()` only inspects `self.requirements`, the
attribute-level Vec. The same struct also carries two enforcement knobs
that were silently skipped whenever that Vec was empty:

* `allowed_verifiers: Vec<String>` — when non-empty, restricts which
  verifier IDs may sign attestations. Skipping the check means an
  attacker-supplied attestation from any verifier is accepted.
* `max_attestation_age_secs: u64` — defaults to 86400 (24h). Even when
  the admin sets a tighter window (e.g. 3600 for 1h), the check is
  skipped. Worse, when no attestation is supplied at all, check_posture
  is the only path that returns `PostureAttestationMissing`; the
  short-circuit lets the request proceed with no attestation.

A zone admin who configured

    requires_posture: Some(PostureRequirements {
        requirements: vec![],                           // no attribute checks
        allowed_verifiers: vec!["intel-tdx".into()],   // but a verifier whitelist
        max_attestation_age_secs: 3600,                // and a 1h freshness ceiling
    })

would have BOTH constraints silently ignored. The intended semantic is
that `Some(_)` is the enforcement signal; once an admin opts in, every
declared constraint must run.

Fix: drop the `if !posture_requirements.is_empty()` guard. The outer
`if let Some(...)` is sufficient. `check_posture` already handles
empty `requirements` correctly — it iterates the (possibly empty) list,
runs verifier-allowlist + age + schema + presence checks, and returns
`Satisfied` only when those pass.

Updated comment cites the trap so a future reader does not re-add the
short-circuit. `PostureRequirements::is_empty()` itself is left alone:
it still says what it says (no attribute requirements) and is called
from no other consumer in the workspace today (rg confirms one-and-only
caller was this site). Renaming or tightening its definition is a
separate API change.

Regression test
`engine_deny_posture_with_empty_requirements_still_runs_check`:

* Builds a `requires_posture: Some(PostureRequirements { ... })` with
  empty `requirements`, an `allowed_verifiers` whitelist, and a
  3600-second `max_attestation_age_secs`.
* Sets `posture_attestation: None` on the decision input.
* Asserts `decision == Deny` with `reason_code ==
  PostureAttestationMissing`. Before the fix this returned `Allow` —
  the worst possible outcome since the missing attestation is exactly
  what posture enforcement is supposed to catch.

Verified locally with rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cc1:
* `cargo test -p fcp-core --lib` → 3912 passed (+1 new), 0 failed
* `cargo clippy -p fcp-core --lib -- -D warnings` → clean

Audit also examined and found clean (reported as cross-review notes,
no patch warranted in this commit):

* `simulate_policy_decision` extracts claims via `claims_unverified()`.
  Production callers in `fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:1280` already run
  `CapabilityVerifier::verify` on the same `request.capability_token`
  before calling simulate, so the unverified read is downstream of an
  already-validated signature in the same scope. The other callers
  (`fwc/src/policy_cmd.rs`, `fcp-testkit` tests, the `preview_*`
  helpers in `fcp-core/src/policy.rs:1961,1968,3175,3179`) are all
  dry-run/preview surfaces that intentionally do not authorize.
* `PolicyBundle::validate` does not recompute `bundle_hash` and compare
  against `self.bundle_hash`. The signature covers both fields, so a
  trusted signer cannot tamper without resigning, but a future swarm
  agent that mutates `policies` post-hoc without recomputing
  `bundle_hash` would slip past `validate`. 17 tests use a placeholder
  `test_bundle_hash()` that doesn't reflect actual contents — wiring
  in a strict consistency check would require updating those test
  fixtures and was out of scope for this commit. Filed as a follow-up
  in the audit notes.
* Pattern-list short-circuits (`check_pattern_lists`,
  `check_transport`, `capability_ceiling`) all run before the posture
  check and were verified to deny on missing/wrong values, not allow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
